zh.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody於1812年出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭。她於1832年12月4日與John Borland III在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭薩萊姆 堂史普結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她於1860年住在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭薩萊姆 堂史普且在美國賓夕法尼亞州阿利格尼皮德次包住了大約10年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody于1812年出生在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭。她于1832年12月4日与John Borland III在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭薩萊姆 堂史普结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她于1860年住在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭薩萊姆 堂史普且在美国宾夕法尼亚州阿利格尼皮德次包住了大约10年。

nameMeanings.name: Dunwoody
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Dinwiddie
meanings: Scottish (Dumfriesshire): habitational name from Dinwoodie near Dumfries. The placename is first recorded in 1296 in the form Dinwithie, Dunwythye, and is probably named with British words that are ancestors of Welsh din ‘forest’ + gwydd ‘shrubs, bushes’.
History: Robert Dinwiddie, born in Glasgow in 1693, was lieutenant governor of VA from 1751 to 1758.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: "Eliza"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Elizabeth  , first used in the 16th century, and popular in the 18th and 19th centuries as an independent given name. It was used by  George Bernard Shaw  for the main female character,  Eliza Dolittle , in his play Pygmalion (  1913 ), which was the basis for the musical and film My Fair Lady.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.description: War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

historicEvents.title: Harrisburg Becomes the State Capital
historicEvents.description: Harrisburg had important parts with migration, the Civil War, and the Industrial Revolution.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History-History of Harrisburg Pennsylvania

historicEvents.description: U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The three day Battle of Gettysburg was one of the bloodiest of the American Civil War. Between the Confederates and Unions, somewhere between 46,000 and 51,000 people died that day.

historicEvents.title: A Treaty with Hawaii
historicEvents.description: In the Mid 1870s, The United States sought out the Kingdom of Hawaii to make a free trade agreement. The Treaty gave the Hawaiians access to the United States agricultural markets and it gave the United States a part of land which later became Pearl Harbor.

historicEvents.title: First National Strike in U.S. Begins In Pittsburgh Against Pennsylvania Railroad
historicEvents.description: Coming out of an economic crisis, everyone was worried when cuts started happening in the railroad. They went on what would the great railroad strike of 1877.

historicEvents.title: Department of Commerce and Labor
historicEvents.description: A short-lived Cabinet department which was concerned with controlling the excesses of big business. Later being split and the Secretary of Commerce and Labor splitting into two separate positions.

historicEvents.title: The Movie Theater
historicEvents.description: The world’s first movie theater was located in Pittsburgh. It was referred to as a nickelodeon as at the time it only cost 5 cents to get in.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody was born in 1812, in Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States. She married John Borland III on 4 December 1832, in Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. She lived in Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States in 1860 and Pittsburgh, Allegheny, Pennsylvania, United States for about 10 years.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody est née en 1812, à Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Elle a épousé John Borland III le 4 décembre 1832, à Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Elle a vécu à Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis en 1860 et à Pittsburgh, Allegheny, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis pendant environ 10 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody wurde 1812 in Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten geboren. Sie hat am 4. Dezember 1832 in Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten John Borland III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1860 hat sie in Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt, und ungefähr 10 Jahre lang hat sie in Pittsburgh, Allegheny, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoodyは1812年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドで生まれた。1832年12月4日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドサレム トウンシュイップでJohn Borland IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1860年にはアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドサレム トウンシュイップに，アメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アレグヘニーピッツブルグに約10年間住んでいた。

ko.Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvan: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 살렘 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody(은)는 1812년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1832년 12월 4일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 살렘 타운시프에서 John Borland III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1860년도에 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 살렘 타운시프에서, 미국 펜실베이니아 주 앨레게이니 피츠버그h에서 약 10년 동안 살았다.

Ela casou-se com John Borland III em 4 de dezembro de 1832, em Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela viveu em Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América em 1860 e em Pittsburgh, Allegheny, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América por quase 10 anos.

ru.Salem Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvan: сеилем тоуншип, вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Mary Elizabeth "Eliza" Dunwoody родилась в 1812 г. в местности вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Borland III 4 декабря 1832 г. в местности сеилем тоуншип, вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Она жила в местности сеилем тоуншип, вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году и в местности питцбург, аллегени, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты около 10 лет.
